title: ISic003434. Epitaph for Cassia Galene
body:
Text encoding: Jonathan Prag; edition source: https://www.zotero.org/groups/382445/items/FZWWPUD6
1D(is) · m(anibus) · s(acrum)·
2Cassia · Galene
3vixit · ann(is) · XXXX
4C(aius) Iulius · Macedo
5uxori · pissiimae
6· fecit ·

Text encoding: Jonathan Prag; edition source: https://www.zotero.org/groups/382445/items/FZWWPUD6
1D·M·S·
2CASSIA·GALENE
3VIXIT·ANN·XXXX
4CIULIUS·MACEDO
5UXORI·PISSIIMAE
6·FECIT·

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text of Bivona and Ferrua (but with interpuncts formally recorded)

English translation

Jonathan Prag

Sacred to the shades of the underworld. Cassia Galene lived 40 years. Caius Iulius Macedo made (this) for his most dutiful wife

commentary

The piece's provenance is uncertain. Ferrua suggested that it should be from Palermo, but it is one of three which he so attributed without much evidence, as one of the three is from Taormina and one from Rome; there appears however to be no documentation regarding the origin of this piece, which Bivona therefore lists among the 'ignota'.

bibliography

  • 1975.0453 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001283
  • Ferrua 1941 267 no.37 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10002655
  • Bivona 1970 88 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10002142

A, M and N all show long extensions of the vertical strokes.

Line 1-6 10-17 Interlineation line 1 to 2
